architecture_guardrails: Preserve one deployable Go binary serving static SvelteKit assets, JSON HTTP, MCP Streamable HTTP, and ingest loop; no sidecar worker, admin, sync, or migration service is authorized by this UI contract.
Preserve one SQLite database with FTS5 lexical retrieval only; no vector DB, embeddings, RAG, semantic answer surface, external database, or sync/merge coordinator is authorized.
Preserve the single owner-token boundary; no accounts, OAuth, registration, password reset, roles, per-agent registry, Settings reset UI, or browser reset control is authorized.
Keep HTTP and MCP product operations equivalent; agents must not receive product concepts unavailable to humans.

Keep backend shape flat if implementation later needs DTO support: direct functions in internal/resofeed, no DI containers, service/repository layers, event buses, plugin registries, or provider abstractions.
Do not change api_runtime by default and do not add product APIs unless an existing DTO demonstrably lacks an item-display-only, source-backed datum recorded as a DTO-gap candidate here.

State portability remains only active sources, active steering rules, and currently resonated items; no command history, reading history, activity ledger, portable receipts, sync controls, merge, or conflict resolver.
UI implementation must remain component composition only; shared abstractions are limited to feed/search item anatomy, metadata formatting, and bracket-action visual contracts.

shared_acceptance_contracts.browser_evidence_for_p0_p1: Every P0/P1 remediation test must assert rendered DOM or browser behavior at the named viewport(s); screenshot-only or prose-only acceptance is insufficient.

checks: Opening a feed/search item moves focus to the Inspector/detail heading without a dominant error-like outline.
Mobile/narrow back action returns focus to the originating item and preserves feed scroll.
Original link is keyboard reachable and Enter opens a real external URL rather than in-app suppression.
Tab reaches [RUN INGEST], row [FETCH], [DELETE], [IMPORT OPML], [EXPORT STATE], and [IMPORT STATE] in reading order.
Enter/Space activates enabled bracket actions; disabled/pending bracket actions remain focus-disciplined, transparent, and do not invert on hover/focus.
Delete confirmation keeps focus in the ledger and returns focus to the next row or ledger heading after deletion.
Narrow/mobile Tab order follows query, source, from, to, resonated, limit, submit, then results.
Labels remain programmatically associated with controls after single-column stacking.
Submitting with Enter preserves focus movement to status/results without trapping keyboard users.
Token input receives initial focus before API calls.
Submit is keyboard reachable and has a 44px hit target without login-form visual weight.
Rejected state keeps input focused and announces err: owner token rejected with aria-live=assertive.
Accepted state moves focus to Steer input or the first feed item.
Mouse click and Enter/Space on original link navigate to the item's source-backed original URL, preferably target=_blank with rel=noopener/noreferrer.
No click/key handler rewrites original link to /#original-link or prevents default navigation.

dto_gap_candidates.reason: Findings 11 and 44 require feed/search fallback text when summary/core_insight is unavailable; ItemSummary currently exposes summary/core_insight but feed_excerpt only exists on ItemDetail. Record as candidate only if implementation cannot derive a source-backed fallback without N+1 detail fetches.

Feed metadata may need agent:<name>; ItemSummary exposes external_surfaced_at but not the surfacing actor label. Record as candidate only when source-backed provenance cannot be displayed from existing fields.

Findings 16, 42, and 44 require search match/provenance verification beyond static text. Existing GET /api/search returns ItemSummary plus query echo, not a match snippet; candidate only if UI cannot produce sufficient source-backed provenance from current fields.

authenticated_feed_items: today full extraction item with summary/core_insight and source-backed original URL
today item with null summary/core_insight and source-backed fallback excerpt candidate
yesterday partial extraction item with warning/status marker
earlier grouped/duplicate story leader and sibling provenance
externally surfaced item requiring agent:<name> display when DTO can provide source-backed actor label
resonated and non-resonated item states for star label/glyph assertions
inspector_items: clean extracted article body with dense summary and why line
dirty corpus body containing nav, related, most popular, advertiser, and commerce strings that must be filtered out of primary reading body
original-unavailable item preserving provenance/status without suppressed original-link behavior
grouped story with every source item reachable through source/provenance disclosure
source_ledger: empty source list with No sources. Paste RSS URL in Steer.
three source rows: ok, rss_fetch_error, and not_fetched/null last_fetch_at
manual ingest idle, pending [FETCHING...], success, and raw error feedback
OPML import result with actual imported/skipped/folders_flattened values
delete confirmation and deletion error state
state_portability: idle Source Ledger footer with [EXPORT STATE] and [IMPORT STATE]
import warning text: import replaces active sources, rules, and stars
exporting/export complete/importing/import complete/import failed raw feedback
search: empty query, loading, no results, partial results, error
result with null summary requiring fallback text candidate
narrow/mobile single-column filter form with query/source/from/to/resonated/limit
owner_token_prompt: rejected err: owner token rejected live region
accepted focus handoff to Steer or first feed item
doctor: command running and command failed raw output

findings.title: Extra masthead chrome above the actual workbench
findings.acceptance: First functional row is the Steer command row with minimal RESOFEED label and no product-positioning subtitle.
required_evidence: Desktop browser DOM asserts no visible masthead/subtitle above command row.
Desktop screenshot: desktop-authenticated-today-split-pane-1280x900.
Narrow browser DOM asserts preview/document chrome is absent above feed.

findings.title: Persistent surface navigation is not part of the design model
findings.acceptance: No persistent dashboard/tab nav strip is rendered for TODAY, SOURCE LEDGER, /doctor, or INSPECTOR.
required_evidence: Desktop and narrow DOM assert absence of nav.surface-nav and tab-like controls.
Browser screenshot proves Source Ledger and /doctor are operational surfaces, not a persistent SaaS tab bar.

findings.title: Mobile/narrow shell keeps too much desktop chrome
findings.acceptance: At <1080px, mobile shows compact single-column feed plus Steer access without masthead, subtitle, nav row, or redundant feed heading crowding the first viewport.
required_evidence: 390x844 browser screenshot shows feed triage in first viewport.
Narrow DOM/visibility assertions prove masthead/subtitle/nav are hidden or absent.
Behavior asserts Steer remains reachable near bottom or fixed command affordance.

findings.title: Responsive breakpoint is too narrow
findings.acceptance: Below 1080px Inspector is a route/full-screen detail view; split-pane Inspector is not visible from 761px through 1079px.
required_evidence: Browser viewport 1079px asserts Inspector split pane hidden and detail uses route/full-screen behavior.
Browser viewport 1080px asserts desktop split pane may render.
Clicking/keyboard-opening an item at 390px moves to detail route/view and back returns to feed.

findings.title: Redundant RESOFEED brand text appears below mobile Steer
findings.acceptance: Mobile command area does not render RESOFEED as footer-like text below Steer.
required_evidence: Narrow DOM/visual check confirms at most one compact brand label and none below Steer.

findings.title: Owner Token Prompt includes extra explanatory copy
findings.acceptance: Prompt anatomy is product label, Enter owner token, token input, submit action, and raw invalid-token line only.
required_evidence: Owner-token screenshot in empty/focused state shows no visible explanatory account/login copy.
Rejected DOM asserts raw err line appears and note is absent.

findings.title: Submit control reads like a generic form button
findings.acceptance: Submit uses terse workbench action treatment with 44px hit target and accessible name, not a wide filled login-style button.
required_evidence: DOM asserts submit accessible name and disabled/enabled states.
Browser bounding box asserts min 44px hit target.
Focused screenshot shows workbench action treatment.

findings.title: Feed has an extra standalone TODAY heading
findings.acceptance: No visible standalone feed time heading; TODAY/YESTERDAY/EARLIER labels only appear inline in group-leading metadata rows with zero added vertical divider height.
required_evidence: DOM asserts #feed-heading is absent or visually hidden for accessibility only without layout space.
Browser layout measurement proves time label lives inside first item metadata row.
Desktop/narrow screenshots show no standalone group divider row.

findings.title: Feed metadata omits item age/time
findings.acceptance: Every feed row metadata includes source, compact age/time, extraction/fallback status, and agent marker when source-backed.
required_evidence: DOM text assertions for multiple rows include src:, compact age such as 2h/1d, and full/partial/excerpt.
Fixture covers published_at null and non-null rows.
Accessibility snapshot includes Source and Extraction names.

findings.title: Feed metadata is uppercased by styling
findings.acceptance: Feed metadata preserves lower-case semantic prefixes like src: and agent: and does not inherit all-caps label styling.
required_evidence: DOM text and computed style check for text-transform not uppercase on feed metadata.

findings.title: Feed summaries show summary unavailable even when detail data has usable text
findings.acceptance: Feed rows render dense summary/core insight, or a cleaned source-backed excerpt fallback instead of repeated summary unavailable when usable text exists.
required_evidence: DOM asserts at least one fixture row with null summary renders excerpt/core-insight fallback.
Browser screenshot shows useful triage text on feed rows.
DTO-gap candidate reviewed before any backend/API change.

findings.title: Desktop feed summary does not clamp to two lines
findings.acceptance: Desktop summaries clamp to two lines; narrow/mobile summaries clamp to one line.
required_evidence: Computed CSS asserts -webkit-line-clamp or equivalent two-line clamp at >=1080px.
Computed CSS asserts one-line clamp at <1080px.
Visual screenshot with long summary confirms row height stays bounded.

findings.title: Title-summary spacing is too loose
findings.acceptance: Feed title-to-summary gap is 4px and does not inherit global paragraph margins.
required_evidence: Computed style/layout measurement confirms 4px gap in desktop and narrow feed rows.

findings.title: Row rhythm is not mathematically stable
findings.acceptance: Feed rows preserve 12px top padding, 11px bottom padding, 1px separator, and no selected/hover/focus/loading layout shift.
required_evidence: Browser bounding-box measurements compare default, selected, hover, focus, long-title, and fallback-summary rows.
DOM/computed styles assert padding/separator values.
Screenshots show stable rhythm on desktop and narrow.

findings.title: Time grouping only handles the first item
findings.acceptance: TODAY, YESTERDAY, and EARLIER are computed from timestamps and applied to each group-leading row only.
required_evidence: Fixture with today/yesterday/earlier items asserts exactly one inline label per group.
Browser DOM order proves labels are inside metadata rows and not standalone dividers.

findings.title: Search results do not reuse feed item anatomy
findings.acceptance: Search results use shared feed-item anatomy plus match/provenance line, selected/inspect behavior, and Resonate action.
required_evidence: Search browser DOM asserts each result has metadata, title, summary/fallback, match/provenance, and star action.
Click and keyboard open result into Inspector/detail.
Screenshot proves search shares feed visual rhythm.

findings.title: Inspector heading focus ring is visually too heavy
findings.acceptance: Programmatically focused Inspector heading receives focus for screen readers without dominant error-like visual outline; keyboard-visible focus remains accessible on interactive controls.
required_evidence: Browser focus test opens Inspector and asserts activeElement is heading.
Computed style/screenshot confirms quiet heading focus treatment.

findings.title: Inspector exposes model status in the visible header
findings.acceptance: Inspector header shows source, extraction status, original link/provenance, and material labels; raw model status is not in primary header.
required_evidence: DOM asserts primary Inspector header excludes model_status strings while /doctor can still expose diagnostics.
Screenshot shows reading header is source/provenance focused.

findings.title: Inspector reading payload contains raw site boilerplate and ads
findings.acceptance: Primary Inspector body renders cleaned editorial text/excerpt without nav, related, most popular, advertiser, commerce boilerplate, or debug scrape artifacts.
required_evidence: Dirty-corpus browser test asserts forbidden boilerplate strings absent from primary reading body.
DOM asserts source-backed readable text remains present.
Desktop and narrow screenshots show editorial payload hierarchy.

findings.title: Inspector information hierarchy is overloaded
findings.acceptance: Primary Inspector flow is provenance header, title, original link/status, dense summary, readable body/excerpt, and concise why line; ranking/search/debug fields are removed from primary path.
required_evidence: DOM asserts internal ranking/search/debug labels are absent from primary Inspector.
Accessibility snapshot confirms source/provenance/original link labels are readable.
Screenshot shows calm reading hierarchy.

findings.title: original link is visibly present but navigation is suppressed
findings.acceptance: Original link has real item URL and browser navigation is not prevented for mouse or keyboard activation; safe external new-tab behavior is allowed.
required_evidence: Browser DOM asserts original link href equals source-backed URL, not /#original-link.
Click behavior observes popup/navigation or target=_blank link semantics without preventDefault suppression.
Keyboard Enter activation has same behavior.

findings.title: Mobile Inspector star is not visible in the first viewport
findings.acceptance: On mobile/single-column Inspector route, Resonate star appears in the top/header action row in the first viewport.
required_evidence: 390x844 browser screenshot shows star near header before reading body.
Bounding-box assertion places star within first viewport and at least 44x44.

findings.title: Raw provenance disclosure copy is too diagnostic-heavy
findings.acceptance: Any provenance disclosure uses calm source/provenance language and raw diagnostics are not in the main reading path.
required_evidence: DOM asserts diagnostic-heavy label absent from visible primary Inspector.

findings.title: Source Ledger does not follow the required DOM contract
findings.acceptance: Source Ledger uses source-ledger block/header/list/row/actions DOM contract and canonical bracket actions or documented equivalent selectors in tests.
required_evidence: DOM test asserts required Source Ledger structural selectors exist.
Browser accessibility snapshot shows list/listitem semantics.
No production backend/API behavior required.

findings.title: Manual ingest actions look like bordered buttons
findings.acceptance: [RUN INGEST] and [FETCH] are transparent bracket-action text controls with enlarged invisible hitboxes, no visible border/radius/shadow, and immediate inversion on hover/focus.
required_evidence: Computed CSS asserts border width 0/transparent and no radius/shadow for bracket actions.
Hover/focus browser test asserts inversion and visible focus.
Bounding boxes assert 44px minimum hit target.

findings.title: Delete action is x instead of [DELETE]
findings.acceptance: Source Ledger destructive action visibly renders [DELETE] and has accessible name Delete source: <name> with confirmation before removal.
required_evidence: DOM asserts no row delete button has visible text x.
Keyboard/mouse behavior opens terse confirmation before deletion.
Accessible name includes source name.

findings.title: Import/export actions are lowercase and not bracket actions
findings.acceptance: Footer/action cluster uses exact labels [IMPORT OPML], [EXPORT STATE], and [IMPORT STATE] with bracket-action styling.
required_evidence: DOM asserts exact uppercase bracket labels and absence of lowercase visible variants.
Keyboard activation works for each action and file input remains reachable.

findings.title: Source Ledger shows a false imported status by default
findings.acceptance: Default Source Ledger status is empty unless a real import occurred; import completion reflects actual result counts.
required_evidence: Initial browser DOM asserts stale imported fixture text absent.
Import fixture behavior asserts completion text uses actual imported/skipped/folders_flattened response.

findings.title: Source rows omit a stable URL column
findings.acceptance: Each source row exposes stable source name/title, URL, last fetch status/diagnostic, and right-aligned action block in deterministic columns.
required_evidence: DOM asserts each fixture row contains source URL text separate from title/status.
Layout/bounding-box assertions prove actions are right-aligned without covering URL on desktop/narrow.

findings.title: last fetch / last ingest labels are not canonical
findings.acceptance: Ledger uses exact canonical labels last_fetch: HH:MM:SS and last_ingest: HH:MM:SS.
required_evidence: DOM text assertions verify underscore labels and reject space-separated variants.

findings.title: File input leaves a visible/occupied artifact
findings.acceptance: OPML file input is keyboard/screen-reader accessible but creates no visible Choose... artifact or layout gap.
required_evidence: Browser screenshot shows no native file control artifact near [IMPORT OPML].
Keyboard test reaches import action and opens/targets file input access path.
Layout measurement confirms hidden input does not occupy action-row geometry.

findings.title: Disabled/active manual controls use filled disabled backgrounds
findings.acceptance: Disabled/pending bracket actions remain transparent, opacity 1, suppress hover/focus inversion, and use raw active text such as [FETCHING...].
required_evidence: Computed CSS for disabled bracket action asserts transparent background and no filled global disabled style leakage.
Hover/focus browser test confirms disabled action does not invert or activate.

findings.title: Source Ledger action block baseline is unstable
findings.acceptance: Header title, last_ingest, and [RUN INGEST] align predictably; row actions align right and expand leftward without baseline drift.
required_evidence: Browser layout measurement across idle/pending/error rows verifies action baselines and right alignment.

findings.title: State Portability is a settings-like separate section
findings.acceptance: State export/import appears only as terse actions in the Source Ledger action cluster/footer; no standalone State Portability settings section is rendered.
required_evidence: DOM asserts standalone State Portability section/heading absent.
Source Ledger DOM asserts [EXPORT STATE] and [IMPORT STATE] present in ledger footer/action cluster.
Screenshot shows no settings-dashboard presentation.

findings.title: State Portability action labels are lowercase and filled
findings.acceptance: State portability actions use exact transparent bracket labels [EXPORT STATE] and [IMPORT STATE].
required_evidence: DOM asserts exact labels and no lowercase filled state portability buttons.
Computed CSS asserts bracket action visual contract.

findings.title: Default State Portability explanatory copy is too verbose
findings.acceptance: Default copy is absent except required import warning shown before file selection/final submit: import replaces active sources, rules, and stars.
selectors: text=import replaces active sources, rules, and stars
required_evidence: DOM asserts verbose bundle explanation absent by default and required warning appears only in import flow.

findings.title: /doctor long lines overflow/crop on narrow viewport
findings.acceptance: Doctor output wraps long lines and never crops text or requires horizontal-only scrolling on mobile.
required_evidence: 390x844 browser test with long openrouter/source/id lines asserts scrollWidth <= clientWidth or wrapping with no clipping.
Computed CSS asserts pre-wrap and overflow-wrap:anywhere or equivalent.
Narrow screenshot shows full lines visible/wrapped.

findings.title: /doctor renders above feed instead of as a clean operational surface
findings.acceptance: /doctor renders as a bounded raw diagnostic operational surface without immediately mixing a long diagnostic block and feed list in the same narrow viewport.
required_evidence: Narrow browser DOM/visibility asserts doctor surface is distinct and feed is not awkwardly interleaved below long diagnostics in first viewport.
Screenshot shows clear diagnostic surface boundaries.

findings.title: /doctor item IDs are visually overwhelming
findings.acceptance: Doctor preserves raw diagnostic strings while wrapping/grouping lines readably and without badges/charts/cards.
required_evidence: DOM/computed style asserts raw pre/log output and wrapping; visual check confirms no dashboard widgets.

findings.title: Search form breaks at narrow width
findings.acceptance: At <1080px, search controls stack in one coherent column with associated labels and no collision or ambiguous wrapping.
required_evidence: 390x844 screenshot shows single-column search form and no text/control collisions.
Browser bounding-box assertions prove controls do not overlap and labels remain associated.
Keyboard Tab order follows visual order.

findings.title: Search title is document-like
findings.acceptance: Search surface uses terse operational label such as SEARCH, not the design-section title Search and Retrieval.
required_evidence: DOM text assertion rejects document-like heading and confirms terse operational label if dedicated surface remains.

findings.title: Search results lack Inspect/Resonate affordances
findings.acceptance: Each search result can be inspected by mouse and keyboard and includes a Resonate action with the same accessible labels/state semantics as feed rows.
required_evidence: Browser behavior test clicks and keyboard-opens search result into Inspector/detail.
DOM asserts star action exists per result with 44px target and state label.
Focus returns appropriately from narrow Inspector back action.

findings.title: Search result dates use raw RFC3339
findings.acceptance: Search results use compact metadata age/date formatting consistent with feed while preserving source-backed provenance.
required_evidence: DOM text assertions reject raw RFC3339 in result metadata and confirm compact age/date token.

findings.title: Search results also show summary unavailable
findings.acceptance: Search results use summary/core insight or cleaned excerpt fallback plus match/provenance line for verification, not repeated summary unavailable.
required_evidence: Fixture DOM asserts null-summary result renders source-backed fallback text and match/provenance line.
Screenshot shows useful result text in desktop and narrow search.
DTO-gap candidate reviewed before any backend/API change.

findings.title: Global button styling conflicts with component contracts
findings.acceptance: Global button styles do not impose filled primary/disabled appearance on bracket actions, owner token actions, stars, or low-chrome text actions.
required_evidence: Computed CSS checks for bracket actions, star, owner submit, and disabled controls prove component-specific styles override global fill.
Browser screenshots cover normal, hover/focus, disabled states.

findings.title: Global focus rule is too broad and too strong
findings.acceptance: Accessible focus remains visible, but programmatic/non-interactive heading focus and specialized controls receive tailored, non-dominant treatment.
required_evidence: Browser focus traversal captures focus-visible styles on interactive controls and quiet style on Inspector heading.
Screenshot confirms focus does not look like error/selection artifact.

findings.title: Accent/focus color appears more often than intended
findings.acceptance: Accent remains reserved for active Resonate; focus color is visible but disciplined and not decorative chrome across the UI.
required_evidence: Visual review screenshots enumerate accent/focus uses and confirm no accent washes or decorative palette leakage.
DOM/computed checks confirm active star uses accent and non-star actions use focus/text semantics as specified.

findings.title: Preview uses non-token hard-coded surface color
findings.acceptance: Preview avoids non-token #fffdf5 for Inspector/mobile Steer background; uses defined DESIGN tokens such as surface/background.
required_evidence: Static contract test searches preview artifact for #fffdf5 and fails if present after remediation.

findings.title: Preview mobile Inspector title size differs from design
findings.acceptance: Preview mobile Inspector title uses DESIGN inspector-title typography: 28px/32px.
required_evidence: Static contract test asserts mobile-detail h2 font-size 28px and line-height 32px after remediation.

findings.title: Preview mobile headers include explanatory labels
findings.acceptance: Preview-only labels mobile feed/mobile inspector are removed from golden product chrome or explicitly annotated as preview-only outside the visual target.
required_evidence: Static contract test asserts product-chrome region excludes explanatory labels or includes preview-only annotation outside target.

findings.title: Preview feed marker/padding model is slightly off-token
findings.acceptance: Preview feed row geometry aligns with DESIGN token padding 12px 12px 11px 0 and non-layout-shifting marker model.
required_evidence: Static/style contract test asserts preview row padding and selected marker do not add layout-shifting left padding.

findings.title: Preview Source Ledger heading level differs from required DOM example
findings.acceptance: Preview and implementation align on Source Ledger heading level/accessible name after spec decision; tests accept only the documented contract.
required_evidence: Static contract test asserts preview Source Ledger heading matches the decided required DOM selector and accessible name SOURCE LEDGER.
